symptoms.early: Brick-red oval to elongated blisters (pustules) on stems and leaves that rupture releasing reddish-brown powdery spores
symptoms.late: Black teliospore pustules in late season, lodging of stems, shriveled grain

treatment.organic: Wettable sulfur; remove barberry bushes from fields
treatment.chemical: Propiconazole 25% EC (1ml/L), Tebuconazole (1ml/L), at first sign of infection
treatment.biological: No effective biological control for field use
treatment.preventive: Rust-resistant varieties, eradicate barberry, prophylactic fungicide in Ug99-risk areas

symptoms.early: White fluffy powdery patches on upper leaf surface of lower leaves
symptoms.late: White-gray coating on all green parts, yellowing and drying of leaves, cleistothecia (black dots) visible in old patches

treatment.preventive: Resistant varieties, avoid dense planting, balanced nitrogen, timely fungicide sprays

symptoms.early: Stripe of yellow-orange uredia arranged in rows along leaf veins on upper leaf surface
symptoms.late: Extensive yellowing of leaves, black teliospores in stripes, reduced grain fill, shriveled kernels

treatment.organic: Wettable sulfur preventively; no effective organic cure
treatment.chemical: Propiconazole 25% EC (1ml/L), Tebuconazole 250 EW (1ml/L), Azoxystrobin + Propiconazole
treatment.preventive: Plant resistant varieties (HD-3226, WH1105), timely sowing to escape high-risk period, prophylactic fungicide at flag leaf stage

symptoms.early: Diseased plants emerge earlier; infected ear with black spore mass instead of grain
symptoms.late: Complete head replaced by black teliospore mass that disperses, leaving bare rachis

treatment.organic: Hot water seed treatment (52°C for 10 minutes)
treatment.chemical: Carboxin + Thiram seed treatment (2g/kg seed), Tebuconazole seed treatment
treatment.preventive: Use certified smut-free seeds, systemic seed treatment fungicides, plant resistant varieties

taxonomy.family_description: Amaranth family; includes beet, spinach, quinoa
Amaranth family; includes beet, spinach, quinoa
